steps.title: Read the narrator's irony
steps.content: The narrator says Sir Malcolm Vale has features suited to a bust or stamp and a forehead that seems full of intelligence. Those details make him look dignified and important, but the praise is undercut by the narrator's sarcasm.

steps.title: Match appearance and doubt
steps.content: The narrator says it would be impertinent to ask too closely about what Vale's half century of public service actually achieved. The comment about his memoirs also jokes that any details came so late they might as well have remained secret. Together, those lines suggest an impressive appearance but uncertain accomplishments.

steps.title: Reject invented claims
steps.content: A invents dishonorable actions. B says Vale served capably and kept a modest profile, but the passage questions his achievements and describes an imposing appearance. D treats the achievements as necessarily secret, while the joke is that they may be unclear or unimpressive. The answer is C.
